primary_use_case: Turn calls, podcasts, videos, and voice notes into transcripts, summaries, articles, newsletters, and social posts.
best_for: Solo operators who already record conversations, interviews, coaching calls, sales calls, or founder notes and want to reuse that raw material.
not_a_fit_if: You do not create audio or video, or you need a full publishing/distribution system instead of repurposing assets.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: ChatGPT with manual transcripts or built-in meeting transcript exports
notes: Strong fit for ForgeCore articles about content repurposing, founder-led marketing, newsletter production, and turning calls into reusable assets. Recommend only when the workflow starts from audio or video input.

primary_use_case: Turn long-form videos into short clips for TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, and LinkedIn.
best_for: Creators, consultants, coaches, and founders with long-form video who need short-form distribution without hiring an editor.
not_a_fit_if: You do not publish video, your source videos are weak, or short-form clips are not part of your customer acquisition channel.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: CapCut or manual clipping inside YouTube Studio
notes: Best used in workflows about content distribution and audience growth. Avoid recommending it as a general AI productivity tool.

primary_use_case: Edit podcasts, screen recordings, courses, and videos with text-based editing and AI cleanup tools.
best_for: Solo creators and small teams that publish audio or video and want faster editing without a full professional editing stack.
not_a_fit_if: You only need basic trimming, or you already use a professional editor and advanced NLE workflow.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: Audacity for audio or CapCut for simple video edits
notes: Good fit for creator workflow articles, podcast-to-newsletter workflows, course production, and screen-recorded SOP production.

primary_use_case: Create lead magnets, social graphics, thumbnails, carousels, simple landing page assets, and brand kits.
best_for: Solo operators who need clean marketing assets quickly without becoming designers.
not_a_fit_if: You need highly custom brand systems, production-grade illustration, or complex design collaboration.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: Canva free plan or Google Slides for basic assets
notes: Strong fit for ForgeCore workflows around lead magnets, simple productized assets, content upgrades, and visual systems.

primary_use_case: Run a newsletter, capture subscribers, send broadcasts, build referral loops, and monetize with newsletter-native tools.
best_for: Creators, newsletter operators, and solo founders who want email publishing plus growth tools in one platform.
not_a_fit_if: You only need a simple contact form, or your business already depends on a full CRM/marketing automation suite.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: Substack or a basic email form connected to MailerLite/Brevo
notes: High strategic fit because ForgeCore itself is newsletter-driven. Use for newsletter growth, creator media business, and owned audience workflows.

primary_use_case: Connect apps and automate repeatable business workflows without custom code.
best_for: Solo operators who need reliable app-to-app automations and want broad integration coverage.
not_a_fit_if: The workflow is very high volume, highly custom, cost-sensitive, or better handled with code.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: Make, n8n, or native app automations
notes: Good default recommendation for beginner-friendly automation workflows. Include cost warnings when task volume can get expensive.

primary_use_case: Build visual multi-step automations across apps with more control over branching, data handling, and scenario logic.
best_for: Builders and operators who want more control than simple trigger-action automations but still prefer no-code/low-code tools.
not_a_fit_if: You want the simplest possible setup, or your team cannot maintain visual automation scenarios.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: Zapier free plan, n8n self-hosted, or native app integrations
notes: Strong fit for deeper ForgeCore workflow articles where operators automate content ops, lead routing, reporting, or internal systems.

primary_use_case: Organize projects, docs, SOPs, content calendars, lightweight CRMs, and operating systems.
best_for: Solo operators and small teams that need one flexible workspace for knowledge, tasks, and simple databases.
not_a_fit_if: You need strict relational database behavior, advanced permissions, or specialized project management at scale.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: Google Docs, Google Sheets, Trello, or Airtable free tiers depending on the workflow
notes: Good fit for operating-system articles, content calendars, SOP libraries, and AI-assisted knowledge bases.

primary_use_case: Research topics, compare sources, find recent information, and summarize web-backed answers.
best_for: Operators who need faster research for content, market scans, tool comparisons, and decision support.
not_a_fit_if: You need guaranteed exhaustive research, legal/medical/financial certainty, or private internal-source analysis.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: Google Search plus manual source review or ChatGPT with browsing where available
notes: Good fit for research workflow articles. Always remind readers to verify important claims against primary sources.

primary_use_case: Research keywords, competitors, SEO opportunities, content gaps, and search visibility.
best_for: Operators who rely on organic search and need structured SEO research beyond free keyword tools.
not_a_fit_if: You are pre-traffic, publishing casually, or cannot turn SEO data into consistent content execution.
free_or_cheaper_alternative: Google Search Console, Google Trends, Ahrefs Webmaster Tools, or free keyword generators
notes: Use in articles about SEO systems, content planning, and competitive research. Avoid pushing it on beginners who do not yet need paid SEO tooling.
